data.title: GE HealthCare reports Q1 2026 revenue $5.13B, Adjusted EPS $0.99, Organic revenue +2.9%
data.description:
GE HealthCare reported first-quarter 2026 results with total revenues of $5.13 billion, up 7.4% year-over-year and organic revenue growth of 2.9%. Adjusted diluted earnings per share were $0.99 and net income attributable to the company was $389 million for the quarter. Management reaffirmed full-year organic revenue guidance while trimming profit and free cash flow outlooks due to pronounced inflationary pressures experienced in the quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $5,131 million for Q1 2026, up 7.4% versus prior year; Organic revenue: $4,916 million, up 2.9%.
  • Gross profit: $1,977 million for Q1 2026 (as reported).
  • Operating income: $515 million for Q1 2026.
  • Net income attributable to GE HealthCare: $389 million; diluted EPS: $0.85.
  • Adjusted diluted EPS: $0.99; Adjusted EBIT: $691 million with Adjusted EBIT margin of 13.5%.

Business Highlights

  • Segment performance: Pharmaceutical Diagnostics grew strongly (PDx revenues $770 million, +21.7% YoY) while Imaging and Advanced Visualization Solutions showed growth; Patient Care Solutions declined year-over-year.
  • Orders and backlog: Total orders were up 1.1% organically with a book-to-bill of 1.07 and backlog of $21.8 billion.
  • Supply-chain and cost impacts: Profitability in the quarter was affected by a discrete PDx supplier issue (now resolved) and notable increases in memory chips, oil and freight costs.
  • Strategic moves and innovation: Closed acquisition of Intelerad to advance cloud-enabled enterprise imaging; announced multiple regulatory clearances and trial milestones including Phase 2/3 LUMINA dosing and FDA clearances for imaging technologies.
  • Capital deployment and balance sheet actions: Completed Intelerad acquisition for $2.3 billion, repurchased 1.4 million shares for $100 million, declared quarterly dividend $0.035 per share, and reported cash and cash equivalents of $2.3 billion with total debt outstanding of $10.1 billion.

Original SEC Filing: GE HealthCare Technologies Inc. [ GEHC ] - 8-K - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Delek US Holdings reports Q1 2026: Net loss $(201.3m), Adjusted EBITDA $211.7m, Adj. EPS $0.08
data.description:
Delek US Holdings reported a first-quarter 2026 net loss attributable to the company of $201.3 million, or $(3.34) per share, while adjusted net income was $4.7 million, or $0.08 per diluted share. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $211.7 million. The company completed a major refinery turnaround and announced refinancing actions to extend revolving credit maturities to 2031 and increase consolidated capacity.

Financial Highlights

  • Net loss attributable to Delek US: $201.3 million for Q1 2026 (total diluted loss per share: $(3.34)).
  • Adjusted net income: $4.7 million, or $0.08 adjusted net income per diluted share for Q1 2026.
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $211.7 million for Q1 2026 (compared with $33.6 million in Q1 2025).
  • Net revenues: $2,653.1 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026.
  • Cash and cash equivalents: $624.1 million as of March 31, 2026; total consolidated long-term debt: $3,183.1 million, yielding consolidated net debt of $2,559.0 million.

Business Highlights

  • Completed the Big Spring refinery turnaround safely, on time and within budget, returning the full system online to capture improved margins ahead of the driving season.
  • Advanced the Enterprise Optimization Plan (EOP), increasing expected annual run-rate cash flow improvements to approximately $220 million from ~$200 million.
  • Delek Logistics progressed midstream operations: drilled its first acid gas injection (AGI) well and continued ramp-up of the Delaware Basin Libby 2 Plant to enhance sour gas processing and handling capabilities.
  • Executed refinancing initiatives: refinanced revolving credit facilities for Delek and Delek Logistics, increasing consolidated borrowing capacity by $300 million and extending revolver maturities to 2031.
  • Announced and completed select asset transactions with Delek Logistics (Tyler tank acquisition closed April 1, 2026; El Dorado tank and terminal asset purchase agreed with expected closing in October 2027).

Original SEC Filing: Delek US Holdings, Inc. [ DK ] - 8-K - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Delek Logistics reports Q1 2026: Net income $32.4M, Adjusted EBITDA $132.3M, reiterates 2026 guidance
data.description:
Delek Logistics reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $32.4 million (diluted net income per unit $0.60) and adjusted EBITDA of $132.3 million. The partnership generated net cash provided by operating activities of $170.4 million and distributable cash flow, as adjusted, of $72.4 million. Management reiterated 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance of $520 million to $560 million and declared a quarterly distribution of $1.130 per unit.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $32.4 million for Q1 2026 (diluted net income per unit $0.60).
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $132.3 million for Q1 2026 (compared with $123.2 million in Q1 2025).
  • EBITDA (GAAP reconciliation): $94.9 million for Q1 2026.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities: $170.4 million for Q1 2026.
  • Distributable cash flow, as adjusted: $72.4 million for Q1 2026; declared quarterly cash distribution of $1.130 per unit (payable May 11, 2026).

Business Highlights

  • Completed drilling of the first acid gas injection (AGI) well at the Libby Gas Complex, advancing sour gas processing, treating and handling capabilities for the Delaware Basin footprint.
  • Continued ramp-up of Delaware crude and water gathering businesses, supporting higher gathering volumes and rising gas gathering & processing volumes despite Winter Storm Fern.
  • Signed a new revolving credit facility that increases borrowing capacity by $150 million and extends maturities to 2031, enhancing liquidity and financial flexibility.
  • Increased third-party EBITDA contributions and progressed economic separation from the sponsor while maintaining commercial alignment.
  • Segment performance: Gathering & Processing adjusted EBITDA increased to $82.9 million; Storage & Transportation adjusted EBITDA rose to $25.2 million driven by sales-type lease income; Wholesale Marketing & Terminalling adjusted EBITDA was $14.3 million, reflecting margin shifts and contract changes.

Original SEC Filing: Delek Logistics Partners, LP [ DKL ] - 8-K - Apr. 29, 2026

data.title: Bunge 1Q Net Down, But Raises 2026 Adjusted EPS View on Grain Prices
data.description:
By Rob Curran

Bunge Global's first-quarter net income fell despite a surge in revenue but the agricultural commodities giant boosted its profit projection for the year as the Iran war drove up the price of grains and production volumes continued to grow.

The St. Louis grain processor and agricultural dealer posted earnings of $68 million, or 35 cents a share, down from $201 million, or $1.48 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-off items, Bunge logged adjusted earnings of $1.83 a share, eclipsing the mean analyst target of 88 cents a share, as per FactSet.

Fourth-quarter sales surged 88% to $21.86 billion, shy of the mean analyst estimate of $23.38 billion, as per FactSet.

Sales at its soybean processing and refining unit billion rose 43% to $9.55 billion, reflecting particular strength in Argentina and Brazil. Softseed processing and refining sales more than doubled to $3.9 billion. Grain merchandising and milling sales rose more than threefold to $7.18 billion. Tropical oils sales rose slightly to $1.23 billion.

Cost of goods sold surged to $21.1 billion from $11.05 billion a year earlier.

Bunge boosted its forecast for 2026 adjusted earnings to a range between $9 and $9.50 a share from a previous projection of $7.50-to-$8 a share, well above the mean analyst estimate of $8.43 a share.

The company's shares have spiked in recent weeks alongside grain prices, which have risen in tune with fertilizer prices. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has bottled up a large portion of the world's fertilizer supply.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Whirlwind Wednesday
data.description:
By Mike Dolan

What matters in U.S. and global markets today

By Mike Dolan, Editor-at-Large, Finance and Markets

Today marks the apex of a packed week of events, with the Federal Reserve’s interest rate decision and earnings reports from four of the U.S. mega-caps. Perhaps unsurprisingly, there’s been some cooling of red-hot stock markets in advance, not least in the supercharged tech and chip space.

That tech retreat was partly triggered by a report that OpenAI had missed some of its internal targets for user growth, calling into question the seemingly boundless optimism around AI demand.

I’ll get into that and more below.

But first, check out my latest column on why a narrowing transatlantic rate gap may be a mirage.

And listen to the latest episode of the Morning Bid daily podcast. Subscribe to hear Reuters journalists discuss the biggest news in markets and finance seven days a week.

WHIRLWIND WEDNESDAY

OpenAI pushed back on the report about missed targets, but its central role in the tech transformation - and its cross-deals with major AI firms - meant the jitters were enough to knock back other stocks, including Oracle and CoreWeave.

All major U.S. indexes closed down on Tuesday and futures were steady before the bell on Wednesday, while Asian markets edged up and European shares rose after the open.

As to the Fed meeting, the stasis in the Iran conflict - which appears to be going nowhere right now - has caused energy prices to edge higher and sent market inflation expectations over the next year or two to six-month highs. No change in rates is expected today. Powell’s guidance is likely to lean hawkish, though it may pack less of a punch than it did over the past eight years.

Markets see less than a 20% chance of another rate cut this year, and March inflation figures due out on Thursday are expected to rise.

Meantime, a worrying rise in household inflation expectations in the euro zone should lead to a more hawkish tilt from the European Central Bank, which meets tomorrow.

Elsewhere, energy markets were digesting Tuesday’s surprise decision by the UAE to leave OPEC after more than 50 years. The move is expected to undermine the cartel’s influence over prices in the longer term, and the UAE is likely to ramp up production once the Iran conflict is over.

But with Gulf oil markets largely frozen thanks to the Iran conflict, there was no immediate crude price reaction, though long-dated futures slipped slightly.

More relevant right now is the continued disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, with Brent and WTI crude spiking on Wednesday to as high as $115 and $103 per barrel, respectively. That was partly on a WSJ report that President Trump has instructed aides to prepare for an extended blockade of Iran.

Chart of the day

The UAE was in February OPEC's fourth‑largest producer after Saudi Arabia, Iran and Iraq - accounting for about 12% of total output, according to the International Energy Agency. The UAE has a capacity of around 4.85 million barrels per day and aims to lift that to 5 million bpd by 2027 - ambitions that sat uneasily with OPEC's ongoing output curbs.

Today's events to watch

* U.S. Federal Reserve interest rate decision (2 p.m. EDT) and news conference with Chair Jerome Powell (2:30 p.m. EDT)

* Bank of Canada interest rate decision (9:45 a.m. EDT)

* U.S. corporate earnings: Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, Qualcomm

Want to receive the Morning Bid in your inbox every weekday morning? Sign up for the newsletter here. You can find ROI on the Reuters website, and you can follow us on LinkedIn and X.

Opinions expressed are those of the author. They do not reflect the views of Reuters News, which, under the Trust Principles, is committed to integrity, independence, and freedom from bias.

data.title: Deutsche Bank's Resilient Performance Dampened By Higher Credit Provisions — Update
data.description:
By Elena Vardon

Deutsche Bank reported a rise in its quarterly profit on the steady performance of its investment bank, though a top-up to its credit loss provisions amid heightened economic uncertainty dragged on its shares.

Germany's largest lender posted an after-tax profit of 2.17 billion euros ($2.55 billion) for the first quarter, 8% higher than a year prior and ahead of consensus expectations of 2.005 billion euros. Total revenue edged up 2% to 8.67 billion euros, surpassing the 8.54 billion-euro result penciled in by analysts in a poll compiled by the bank.

Growth at its retail banking arm and at DWS, its separately listed asset-management arm, offset declines at its corporate bank and a subdued result at its investment bank--the firm's primary revenue engine.

Investment-banking profit was softer on a flat top-line, as expected. The division recorded higher activity at its debt and equity origination business and a stable performance in its large fixed-income division--which includes currency and bond trading--due to negative foreign-exchange impacts and a difficult comparison against a record quarter a year ago.

Costs fell 2% to 5.11 billion euros for the quarter due to efficiency gains. However, the bank set aside 519 million euros to cover bad loans, a 10% increase over the first quarter of the previous year. Management described the provisioning as a prudent response to increased macroeconomic uncertainties linked to the Middle East conflict and a specific commercial real-estate exposure within the investment bank.

Shares slid around 2% on Wednesday as investors digested the print.

Concerns around surging energy prices threaten to temper Germany's economic growth expectations--a cornerstone of the bank's latest business plan, which aims to capture tailwinds from the country's multiyear spending spree on infrastructure and defense.

"When it comes to Germany, we want to reiterate that despite lower growth estimates in 2026, our medium-term view is unchanged," Chief Executive Christian Sewing told analysts.

After a period of restructuring aimed at stabilizing operations and moving past legacy scandals, the bank outlined a road map to boost profitability and slash costs through 2028 with a set of new targets.

"This quarter's record profit gives us a great start on the next phase of our strategy," Sewing said, confirming the bank's outlook for the year, which includes a 33 billion-euro revenue target.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: GE HealthCare Technologies 10-Q: Revenue $5,131M, EPS $0.85
data.description:
GE HealthCare Technologies reported quarterly revenue of $5,131 million, up 7.4% year-over-year, while diluted earnings per share fell to $0.85 from $1.23 a year earlier, reflecting lower net income and margin pressure. The company cited product and service growth driving revenue gains, but cost inflation, tariffs and increased design investments weighed on profitability. Net income attributable to GE HealthCare was $389 million, contributing to the EPS decline.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $5,131 million, up 7.4% year-over-year (organic +2.9%).
  • Gross profit: $1,977 million; margin compression driven by cost inflation and incremental tariffs.
  • Operating income: $515 million, down 18.2% (operating margin 10.0% versus 13.2% prior year).
  • Net income: $411 million; net income attributable to GE HealthCare $389 million, decreased 31.0% year-over-year.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.85, compared with $1.23 in the prior year period.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Product and service growth supported total revenue increase; PDx business grew 21.7% (organic 9.7%), aided by the NMP acquisition.
  • Geographic performance: Strength noted in USCAN and EMEA regions; China declined by approximately 4.4%, impacted by local competition and volume-based procurement pressure.
  • Portfolio and M&A: Completed the Intelerad acquisition, supporting imaging and AVS expansion and contributing to portfolio momentum.
  • Operational investments: Expanded manufacturing and capacity investments and increased design follow-through expenditures, which raised product costs.
  • Services: Growth in service contracts bolstered services revenue.

Original SEC Filing: GE HealthCare Technologies Inc. [ GEHC ] - 10-Q - Apr. 29, 2026
